title: Helix CLI: Ticket Resolution, Help Handling, JSON Output & Inspection Ergonomics
description: This tour walks through a multi-defect fix in helix-cli that addresses five root causes: (1) raw ticket ID passthrough causing HTTP 404s for short IDs and numeric references, (2) missing --help interception at global/router/command levels, (3) absent --json output mode for agent consumption, (4) Invalid Date rendering for run timestamps, and (5) PowerShell-unsafe SQL input in inspect db. The fix introduces a shared client-side ticket resolver (fetch-list-then-match), a shared help-detection utility, --json flags on list/get, a safe formatDate helper, and a --query flag for inspect db — all with 30 unit tests and zero new dependencies.

steps.description: Foundation layer. This file gains the `isHelpRequested` utility plus shared helpers `hasFlag`, `getFlag`, and `getPositionalArgs`. All three levels of help interception (global, router, per-command) depend on `isHelpRequested` existing here first. Without a central utility, every command would need its own ad-hoc argv scan, which was the root cause (RC-2) of --help flags either erroring or silently executing command logic instead of printing usage.
steps.title: Step 1 — Shared flag utilities and isHelpRequested

steps.description: New module implementing the fetch-list-then-match pattern (mirroring the proven `resolve-repo.ts`). `matchTicket` performs three-tier matching in priority order: (1) exact internal CUID match against `item.id`, (2) case-insensitive short ID match against `item.shortId` (e.g. 'BLD-339'), (3) numeric suffix match extracting the number from `item.shortId` (e.g. '339' matches 'BLD-339'). Ambiguity produces an explicit error listing all matches and the org name; not-found produces an error showing the input ref and org name. This directly fixes RC-1: the old `resolveTicketId()` returned raw input unchanged, which the API rejected with 404 for any non-CUID format.
steps.title: Step 2 — Core shared ticket resolver

steps.description: Lines 117-126 emit the two user-facing error paths. The ambiguous-match error lists all candidate tickets and names the org so the user can supply a more specific reference. The not-found error echoes back the exact input ref plus org name so the user knows which org was searched. This satisfies AC-9 (unresolved references fail with clear errors including org context) and the implementation principle 'Fail clearly, never silently'.
steps.title: Step 2b — Ambiguity and not-found error messages with org context

steps.description: The top-level entry point gains a `configOrHelp()` guard so that `hlx --help` and `hlx -h` print usage and exit before any config loading or API calls. RC-2 identified that the main switch had no early-exit for the help flag at this level; users got config errors or unexpected behavior instead of usage text. The change also ensures the help output is written to stdout (not stderr) for correct pipe and pager behaviour.
steps.title: Step 3 — Global --help/-h interception

steps.description: The router replaces the old `resolveTicketId()` raw-passthrough (lines 13-26) with `extractTicketRef` + `resolveTicket` from `resolve-ticket.ts`. A single resolution call now serves all six ticket-ref subcommands (get, artifacts, artifact, rerun, continue, bundle). Lines 35-37 add router-level help interception; lines 57-59 add per-command help checks. This is the highest-leverage change: one refactor eliminates 404s across every ticket subcommand simultaneously.
steps.title: Step 4 — Ticket router refactoring: adopt shared resolver + per-command help

steps.description: Lines 32-44 replace unsafe `new Date(rawString).toLocaleString()` with a `formatDate` helper that validates the Date object with an `isNaN` check before formatting. If the date is invalid it returns the raw string rather than 'Invalid Date'. The helper also cross-references `runStatus` to omit finish timestamps for runs that have not yet completed. This fixes RC-4 (Invalid Date rendering) and satisfies AC-8.
steps.title: Step 5a — formatDate: safe date validation and terminal status cross-reference

steps.description: Lines 85-94 check for the `--json` flag and, when present, emit `JSON.stringify` of the full ticket object with the untruncated description field. The existing human-readable text output remains the default, preserving backward compatibility. This satisfies AC-6 and the 'additive, not destructive' implementation principle.
steps.title: Step 5b — --json output for tickets get

steps.description: Lines 90-93 add `--json` output mode for `tickets list`, emitting all `TicketItem` fields including the internal `id` field that agents need for subsequent API calls. The text output path is also updated to display the internal ID alongside the short ID, satisfying AC-5 and the agent-consumption requirements of the product spec.
steps.title: Step 6 — --json output for tickets list + internal ID in text output

steps.description: A help interception guard is added before the command's API call sequence. Previously `hlx tickets latest --help` would execute the full network request and then print or fail. The guard satisfies AC-7 (help flags work at all three levels without API calls) for this subcommand.
steps.title: Step 7a — Help check in tickets latest

steps.description: Same pattern: `isHelpRequested` guard added before validation logic so that `hlx tickets create --help` exits cleanly without triggering required-field errors. Fixes the RC-2 manifestation in the create command.
steps.title: Step 7b — Help check in tickets create

steps.description: This command compared `positional[0] === ticketId` to strip the ticket reference from the continuation context args. After resolution, `ticketId` is the internal CUID while `positional[0]` is still the raw user input (e.g. '339'), so the comparison always failed and the raw ref leaked into the continuation payload. The fix passes the raw reference as a fourth `rawRef` parameter so the filter uses the original string. Help interception is also added before any validation.
steps.title: Step 8 — continue.ts rawRef parameter for positional arg filtering

steps.description: The comments router contained a duplicate `resolveTicketId()` (lines 6-14) with identical raw-passthrough logic — the same RC-1 defect. This block is removed and replaced with the shared `resolveTicket` call from `resolve-ticket.ts`. Help handling is also added. Removing the duplicate ensures there is exactly one ticket resolution code path in the codebase, eliminating drift risk.
steps.title: Step 9 — Remove duplicate resolveTicketId and adopt shared resolver

steps.description: Lines 57-59 add a `--query` / `-q` flag as an alternative to positional SQL arguments. On PowerShell, passing SQL containing Postgres double-quoted identifiers (e.g. `SELECT "id" FROM ...`) as positional args causes quoting failures. The `--query` flag accepts the SQL as a named string value, sidestepping shell quoting rules. Updated usage examples include PowerShell-safe invocations. Help checks are also added for all inspect subcommands. Satisfies AC-11.
steps.title: Step 10 — --query flag for inspect db and PowerShell-safe examples

steps.description: Tests for `matchTicket` cover internal ID exact match, short ID case-insensitive match, numeric suffix match, ambiguity detection, empty list, and priority ordering. Tests for `extractTicketRef` cover --ticket flag, HELIX_TICKET_ID env var, and positional arg fallback. Tests for `isHelpRequested`, `hasFlag`, `getFlag`, and `getPositionalArgs` are in `flags.test.ts`. All 30 tests run via `node:test` (Node >= 18 built-in), zero new dependencies. The `test` script is wired into `package.json`. Satisfies AC-10.
steps.title: Step 11 — Unit tests: 30 tests covering all resolution paths

steps.description: The `test` script entry added here runs `node:test` against the two new test files. This is the final integration checkpoint: `npm test` verifies all 30 unit tests pass, `tsc --noEmit` confirms typecheck passes across all 14 changed files. Together these gates confirm that the shared resolver, flag utilities, --json flags, formatDate, rawRef fix, duplicate removal, and inspect ergonomics all compose correctly with no regressions in unmodified code paths.
steps.title: Step 12 — Test script and build verification
